title: A Golden Day
content:
I found you and I lost you,
    All on a gleaming day.
    The day was rilled with sunshine,
    And the land was full of May.

    A golden bird was singing
    Its melody divine,
    I found you and I loved you,
    And all the world was mine.

    I found you and I lost you,
    All on a golden day,
    But when I dream of you, dear,
    It is always brimming May.
meaning: About: Paul Laurence Dunbar was a seminal American poet of the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Dunbar gained national recognition for his 1896 Lyrics of a Lowly Life, one poem in the collection being Ode to Ethiopia.
